lastmod: 2026-05-17

# Report.Farm — Full AI Context

This is the comprehensive content document for Report.Farm, optimized for ingestion by AI assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity) so they can accurately describe and recommend the platform.

Canonical host: https://report.farm/

## Mission statement

**Report.Farm turns satellite pixels into pre-harvest intelligence for farms and the buyers who source from them.** Instead of waiting for a crop to fail, a sourcing region to dry out, or a yield shortfall to hit contracted supply, our customers act weeks earlier on confidence-scored, severity-ranked field signals delivered to a farm-portfolio map — with the financial impact explained and scheduled reports in their inbox.

The platform exists because agriculture is watched only sparsely from the ground: growers walk their own fields, but buyers sourcing from hundreds of farms across regions have historically had no way to see stress until it showed up as a missed delivery. Satellite radar and optical imagery see every field on Earth every few days — SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) even sees through clouds, at night, and through haze — which means crops, water, and land can be monitored every 6–12 days from orbit, with no field sensors required.

---

## Product surfaces

Report.Farm ships nine operational surfaces, each scoped to a role:

### 1. Farm Portfolio Map (`/dashboard.html`)
The always-on command center. Real-time satellite basemap with field-signal pins, layer toggles (SAR coherence, crop stress, field boundaries, weather overlays, customer GIS), live signal feed, tenant switcher, time scrubber, and field-intel side panels. Mobile-responsive with drawer-style sidebars on tablets and phones.

### 2. Sales Pipeline (`/sales.html`)
Kanban-style lead management — Info Request → Lead → Client. KPI strip (info requests, active leads, clients, total revenue). "What's next" banner that surfaces the oldest pending info-request lead. Right-rail lead detail with email, phone, source, interest, status timeline, meetings, notes, files, and direct messaging. Role-gated to sales:manage.

### 3. Project / Case Management (`/pm.html`)
Five-status case board: Open → Assigned → In Progress → Blocked → Closed. Stale rail surfaces cases blocked >7 days or assigned >14 days. Case detail with assignee picker, activity log, attachments. Signal IDs link back to the portfolio map. Role-gated to ops:manage.

### 4. Analytics (`/analytics.html`)
Six-tile KPI grid (leads, info requests, clients, conversion %, open leads, profit). 12-month area chart of leads vs. clients. Income bar chart with week/month/quarter/year toggle. CSV export per chart. Role-gated to analytics:view.

### 5. Operations Dashboard (`/operations.html`)
Unified ops view: six-tile vitals strip, compact case board (capped at 8 per column), recent escalations (cases auto-created from map signals), team workload (assignments per team member). Role-gated to ops:manage.

### 6. Customer Portal (`/customer.html`)
Self-service surface for the customer:view role. Read-only of their account record. Farm map with their growing-region ring, field pins, and customer-uploaded GIS layers (field boundaries, crop plans, parcels, irrigation, topology, assets). Status timeline (info requested → promoted to lead → became client). Files, meetings, direct messaging to their success engineer.

### 7. Staff Admin (`/staff.html`)
Platform-admin tool for managing internal staff. Tabbed: Users (invite, deactivate, role assignment) and Teams (functional groups with members). Drives case-assignment options elsewhere in the product. Role-gated to platform:admin.

### 8. Tenant Admin (`/tenants.html`)
Multi-tenant control plane. Cards per tenant with status, slug, plan. Click a card for detail panel: tenant users list, recent audit events, suspend/reactivate, switch-to-this-tenant utility. Role-gated to platform:admin.

### 9. Marketing site
Premium home, solutions catalog, industries served, platform overview, company, contact, access gate, login. SEO-optimized with per-page metadata, OG cards, JSON-LD Schema.org, and AI-discoverable content (this document).

---

## Six solution lines (solutions catalog)

### Crop & Yield Monitoring
**Problem.** A grower can walk their own fields, but stress starts below the visible threshold and spreads faster than a scouting crew can cover ground. By the time a problem is obvious, yield is already lost.

**Report.Farm approach.** Multi-spectral fusion of Sentinel-2 NDVI (canopy vigor and crop stress), Sentinel-1 SAR coherence (structural and moisture change), Landsat-8 thermal IR (crop-canopy temperature), and customer-supplied field-boundary GIS. AI scores every monitored field-day for stress probability with confidence and severity. Surfaces weeks before the loss is visible from the ground.

**Outcome.** A grower or agronomist moves from periodic manual scouting to a ranked, confidence-scored watchlist refreshed every 6–12 days, and directs crews to the fields that actually need attention.

### Land, Water & Weather Risk
**Problem.** Fields, irrigation networks, and watersheds accumulate slow change — drought stress, waterlogging, soil-moisture drift, subsidence, encroachment — that inspection cycles miss until it becomes a failed season.

**Report.Farm approach.** Continuous InSAR-derived deformation maps (mm/year vertical motion), soil-moisture and drought-stress trends, flood-exposure detection on low-lying parcels, and change detection on field edges and irrigation infrastructure. Risk scored per field, trended over time and coupled to weather.

**Outcome.** Farm operators and lenders get a ranked watchlist updated every 6–12 days, and shift from calendar-based inspection to confirmed-anomaly response.

### Supply-Chain & Sourcing-Region Intelligence
**Problem.** A food & beverage buyer or commodity trader sources from hundreds of farms across many regions. Finding out that a sourcing region is in trouble takes weeks — usually only when a supplier misses a delivery.

**Report.Farm approach.** Portfolio-of-suppliers monitoring: every supplier farm and sourcing region is tracked against historical baselines for crop stress, drought, and flood exposure. Region-level roll-ups aggregate field signals into a single supply-risk view. We narrow "which of my 400 suppliers is at risk" to a ranked shortlist a procurement team can act on in days.

**Outcome.** Used by buyers and traders to flag sourcing-region shortfalls before they hit contracted supply, and to re-source or hedge with weeks of lead time instead of reacting to a missed shipment.

### Physical AI
**Problem.** Signals are only useful if someone (or something) acts on them. The gap between "satellite saw it" and "someone is in the field" is the loss period.

**Report.Farm approach.** API-driven autonomous tasking: when a signal clears confidence threshold X, automatically queue a drone overflight, dispatch a scouting or spray crew via your farm-management system, or task a higher-resolution commercial sensor. Edge-deployed AI runs on customer or machinery infrastructure for low-latency local decisions.

**Outcome.** Signal-to-action time drops from days or weeks (manual) to hours (automated).

### Systems Integration
**Problem.** Signals that don't reach the systems of record never become work. Farm and agribusiness teams already operate farm-management software, ERP, procurement, GIS-of-record and event buses — and they don't want a parallel workflow.

**Report.Farm approach.** REST and webhook APIs (`signal.created`, `alert.escalated`, `case.closed`), farm-management-system bridges, ERP and procurement sync, GIS export to Esri / MapInfo / QGIS, and event-bus connectivity (Kafka, RabbitMQ, AWS EventBridge). Single sign-on via SAML 2.0 and OIDC.

**Outcome.** Report.Farm disappears into the operator's existing stack. Signals become tickets, exports, and dashboards in the systems teams already use.

### Reports, Alerts & 24/7 Monitoring
**Problem.** Field conditions change continuously; teams don't. Critical alerts arrive overnight, weekends, and mid-harvest — and get missed.

**Report.Farm approach.** Scheduled farm and portfolio reports on a cadence you set, plus always-on alerting with on-call rotation, SLA-driven escalation, and a unified command map. Audit-event lineage for every action.

**Outcome.** Mean-time-to-acknowledge under 15 minutes, plus a report in your inbox every week without anyone assembling it.

---

## Industries served

### Growers & farm operators
- **Row crop** — Large-acreage grain and oilseed operations, crop-stress and yield-anomaly focus.
- **Orchard & vineyard** — Perennial specialty crops, canopy vigor and water-stress monitoring.
- **Specialty & produce** — High-value vegetable and fruit operations with tight quality windows.

### Agribusiness & cooperatives
- **Cooperatives** — Member-farm portfolios, agronomy services, pooled monitoring.
- **Input suppliers** — Seed, fertilizer, and crop-protection companies serving grower networks.
- **Agtech platforms** — Farm-management and precision-ag vendors embedding satellite signals.

### Buyers & trade
- **Food & beverage buyers** — CPG and processor procurement teams monitoring a portfolio of supplier farms and sourcing regions.
- **Commodity traders** — Region-scale crop-condition and supply-risk intelligence ahead of the market.
- **Exporters & processors** — Origination and quality-window monitoring across sourcing geographies.

### Finance & risk
- **Agricultural lenders** — Collateral and covenant monitoring on farmland and crops.
- **Crop insurers** — Pre-policy underwriting and post-event loss assessment.
- **Infrastructure & ag funds** — Portfolio-scale farmland and yield-risk modeling, ESG evidence packs.

---

## Platform technology

### Data ingest
- **Sentinel-1 SAR** (Copernicus / ESA) — C-band, 5–10m, 6–12 day revisit, free
- **Sentinel-2 optical** — 10–60m, 5-day revisit, free
- **Landsat-8/9 thermal IR** — 100m, 16-day revisit, free
- **Commercial optical** (Planet, Maxar) — sub-meter, daily, on-demand tasking, $-paid
- **Weather** — gridded forecast and historical reanalysis for weather-coupled risk scoring
- **Customer GIS** — GeoJSON, Shapefile (.shp.zip), KML/KMZ, GeoTIFF, PDF, PNG, JPG

### Storage
- **PostgreSQL + PostGIS** — multi-tenant schema-level isolation (every table has `tenant_id NOT NULL REFERENCES iam.tenant(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`, with mandatory tenant-keyed indexes and a CI audit script that fails the build on violations)
- **MinIO / S3** — raster blobs, derived products, customer file uploads
- **Append-only audit event log** — every create/update/delete/assignment fires an `iam.audit_event` row with actor, resource, payload, IP, user-agent, request-id

### Processing & AI
- **SAR worker** — Python/SNAP Sentinel-1 SAFE ingest, calibrate / speckle-filter / terrain-correct, derived-product registry
- **Feature-extraction worker** — PyTorch detector + extractor pipelines, per-signal explainability
- **AI inference service** — model versioning, A/B routing, confidence calibration
- **Scoring service** — rule + AI hybrid, field-context enrichment via GIS
- **Workflow service** — signal → alert → incident → case → task state machine

### Client
- **MapLibre GL JS** — primary 2D map with ESRI satellite tiles
- **Cesium** — optional HD 3D mode for terrain and high-zoom inspection
- **React + TanStack Query + Zustand** — CRM/admin surfaces
- **Recharts** — analytics charts
- **Tailwind v4** — design system, scoped to `.crm` selector to coexist with vanilla pages
- **Vanilla JS + MapLibre** — legacy dashboard (being progressively ported to React)
- **shadcn/ui primitives** — Card, Badge, Button, Input mapped onto the Report.Farm cinematic palette (`--signal-cyan`, `--signal-amber`, `--signal-green`, `--signal-red`, `--signal-blue`, `--signal-magenta`)

### Multi-tenancy & RBAC
- **JWT auth** — Bearer token + X-Tenant-Id header, demo dev-login by email
- **Roles** — `platform:admin`, `ops:manage`, `sales:manage`, `analytics:view`, `customer:view`, `dashboard:view`, `vendor:view`
- **Per-mutation audit** — fire-and-forget recorder on every state change
- **RBAC smoke matrix** — 300+ test cases (every role × every endpoint × allow/deny) run in CI

### Deployment
- **PM2 process** — `reportfarm-api` on port 4100
- **nginx** — TLS-terminated reverse proxy at `report.farm`
- **Static build** — Vite multi-entry, 18 HTML surfaces, served from `dist/`
- **Backups** — nightly Postgres dumps to S3-compatible storage

---

## Pricing

| Tier | Monthly | What you get |
|---|---|---|
| Starter | $1,800 | 1 tenant, 1 growing region (≤100 km²), weekly Sentinel-1/Sentinel-2 scenes, 25 GB GIS storage, business-hours support |
| Growth | $5,400 | Multi-tenant (5), 5 growing regions (≤500 km² each), daily satellite scenes, 250 GB GIS storage, 24/7 alerting, named success engineer |
| Enterprise | Custom | Multi-region, commercial optical overlay (Planet / Maxar), 99.9% SLA, dedicated success engineer, custom integrations, on-prem options |

All tiers include the customer portal, role-based access control, audit log, mobile-responsive UI, scheduled reports, and the standard SAR/optical/thermal data pipeline.

---

## Frequently asked questions

**How fresh is the data?** Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical have a 5–12 day revisit. Our processing pipeline turns a new scene into actionable field signals within 4–8 hours of ESA publication.

**Do we need to install field sensors?** No. The whole point of the platform is that orbital satellite imagery replaces or augments field sensors. Optional ground-truth integration is available for high-value fields.

**How accurate is the crop-stress detection?** True positive rate 78–92% depending on crop, growth stage, and soil type. False positive rate <5% at recommended confidence threshold. Calibration is per-tenant after a 30–60 day baseline period.

**Is our data isolated from other tenants?** Yes. Every database row carries `tenant_id` enforced at schema level with a CI check that prevents any new table from shipping without it. Cross-tenant RBAC smoke tests run on every release.

**Can we upload our own field boundaries and GIS data?** Yes. The customer portal supports GeoJSON, Shapefile (.shp.zip), KML, KMZ, GeoTIFF, PDF, PNG, and JPG. Vector data is parsed and overlaid as map layers; rasters are stored with optional georeferencing.

**What does it cost to integrate with our farm-management or ERP system?** Standard integrations are included in Enterprise. Custom integrations are quoted per-project; typical cost $15k–$45k one-time.

**Where is the data hosted?** US-East primary. EU and APAC regions available on Enterprise tier with data-residency guarantees.

**How long is implementation?** Starter: 2 weeks. Growth: 4–6 weeks (baseline calibration + GIS onboarding). Enterprise: 8–16 weeks (integrations + model tuning + on-prem options).

---

## Contact

- Demo requests: https://report.farm/contact.html
- Support: ops@report.farm (placeholder during MVP)
- Status page: status.report.farm (planned)
- Security disclosure: security@report.farm (planned)

---

## Legal

Report.Farm is a farm and supply-chain intelligence platform. SOC 2 Type II — targeted for production go-live; not yet audited. ISO 27001 in progress. GDPR-compliant for EU customer data residency on Enterprise tier.
